response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be mindful of the sources of information and verify the authenticity of posts before sharing them. This can be done by checking the profile of the poster, looking for fact-checking websites or sources, and using reverse image search tools to identify manipulated images. Additionally, setting up accounts with privacy settings that limit who can contact you and what kind of content you see can help reduce exposure to fake content. Furthermore, using third-party fact-checking tools such as Snopes or FactCheck.org can provide an extra layer of protection against misinformation. Finally, it is important to be aware of the potential consequences of spreading false information and to take responsibility for the content we share on social media platforms."
,
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to be cautious when engaging with posts. This involves verifying the authenticity of posts by checking profiles and fact-checking websites, using reverse image search tools, and setting up privacy settings for reduced exposure. Additionally, third-party fact-checking tools can provide an extra layer of protection against misinformation. By taking these steps, individuals can reduce their vulnerability to fake content on social media platforms."
,
"tone": "informative"

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be mindful of the sources of information and verify the authenticity of posts before sharing them. This can be done by checking the profile of the poster, looking for fact-checking websites or sources, and using reverse image search tools to identify manipulated images. Additionally, setting up accounts with privacy settings that limit who can contact you and what kind of content you see can help reduce exposure to fake content. Furthermore, using third-party fact-checking tools such as Snopes or FactCheck.org can provide an extra layer of protection against misinformation. Finally, it is important to be aware of the potential consequences of spreading false information and to take responsibility for the content we share on social media platforms.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to be cautious when engaging with posts. This involves verifying the authenticity of posts by checking profiles and fact-checking websites, using reverse image search tools, and setting up privacy settings for reduced exposure. Additionally, third-party fact-checking tools can provide an extra layer of protection against misinformation. By taking these steps, individuals can reduce their vulnerability to fake content on social media platforms.
